title: NifContext infrastructure, WorkflowHandle interior mutability, and NIF entry registration expansion
purpose: Establish the foundation every NIF bridge brief depends on: a NifContext that resolves the calling BEAM process to its workflow's durability context (Recorder + Resolver), interior-mutable Recorder access on WorkflowHandle, and expanded NIF entry registration for all 18 aion_flow_ffi functions. Without this, no NIF can reach the durability layer and no side effect is recorded or replayed.
task: Create nif_context.rs with the NifContext struct that resolves a calling PID to a WorkflowHandle via the Registry, provides synchronous Recorder access from a dirty NIF thread via tokio Handle::block_on (D2), and carries a Resolver for replay checks (CO1). Expand engine_nifs.rs to register NIF entries for all 18 aion_flow_ffi functions (stubs for NB-002 through NB-008 that return {error, not_yet_implemented}). Confirm WorkflowHandle's existing Arc<Mutex<Recorder>> wrapping (D7) is sufficient for the NIF bridge's interior-mutable access pattern and add any missing accessor methods.

requirements.title: NifContext PID-to-handle resolution
requirements.spec: WHEN a dirty NIF is invoked, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a NifContext from the calling process's PID by looking up the WorkflowHandle in the active-execution Registry (D1). IF no handle exists for the PID, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return an error term to the BEAM process without panicking.
acceptance: nif_context.rs defines NifContext with a constructor that takes a PID, a reference to the Registry, and a tokio Handle.
The constructor resolves the PID to a WorkflowHandle by scanning the Registry's active handles; returns Result<NifContext, NifContextError> where the error case is UnknownProcess.
NifContext exposes workflow_id(), run_id(), and recorder() accessors derived from the resolved handle.
A unit test constructs a Registry with a registered handle, resolves the PID to a NifContext, and asserts the workflow_id matches; a second test uses an unregistered PID and asserts UnknownProcess.

requirements.title: Synchronous Recorder access from dirty NIF thread
requirements.spec: WHEN the NifContext needs to record or resolve events, THE SYSTEM SHALL use tokio Handle::block_on to run async Recorder methods synchronously from the dirty NIF thread (D2). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT spawn async tasks that outlive the NIF call.
acceptance: NifContext carries a tokio::runtime::Handle and exposes a block_on_recorder() or equivalent method that locks the Arc<Mutex<Recorder>> and executes an async closure synchronously via Handle::block_on.
The method returns the closure's Result, propagating DurabilityError as NifContextError::Durability.
A test verifies that a Recorder method (e.g. current_head()) can be called synchronously through the NifContext's block_on path without deadlock.
No async task is spawned that outlives the NIF call scope.

requirements.title: WorkflowHandle Recorder accessor sufficiency
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL expose on WorkflowHandle all accessor methods the NIF bridge requires: recorder() returning Arc<Mutex<Recorder>>, workflow_id(), run_id(), and pid(). IF any accessor is missing, THE SYSTEM SHALL add it to handle.rs.
acceptance: WorkflowHandle::recorder() returns Arc<Mutex<Recorder>> (already present — verify no changes needed).
WorkflowHandle::workflow_id(), run_id(), and pid() are all pub and return the expected types.
The NifContext's resolved handle provides all fields the downstream NIF briefs (NB-002 through NB-009) need without breaking encapsulation.
No new pub(crate) field is added that was not already part of the handle's API surface.

requirements.title: Expanded NIF entry registration for all 18 FFI functions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL register NIF entries in engine_nifs.rs for all 18 aion_flow_ffi functions declared by the Gleam SDK. WHEN a not-yet-implemented NIF is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return {error, <<"not_yet_implemented">>} to the BEAM process.
acceptance: engine_nif_entries() returns a Vec<NifEntry> with 18 entries covering: run_activity/3, now/0, random/0, random_int/2, sleep/1, start_timer/2, cancel_timer/1, with_timeout/2, receive_signal/2, send_signal/3, register_query/3, reply_query/2, dispatch_query/2, spawn_child/3, await_child/1, collect_all/2, collect_race/2, collect_map/2.
Each stub NIF returns {error, <<"not_yet_implemented">>} as a BEAM term.
run_activity/3 retains its existing dirty registration; all other NIFs are registered dirty (they may block on service calls via block_on).
A test asserts engine_nif_entries().len() == 18 and that each MFA is unique.

requirements.title: Replay-check contract for every NIF
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide on NifContext a resolve_command() method (or equivalent) that wraps the Resolver's resolve() call so every NIF can check recorded history before executing a side effect (CO1). WHEN the Resolver returns a recorded result, THE SYSTEM SHALL return it to the NIF without executing the live path.
acceptance: NifContext exposes a method that accepts a Command and returns ResolveOutcome (Recorded or ResumeLive).
The method delegates to a Resolver constructed from the workflow's recorded history via the HistoryCursor.
NifContext construction builds or receives the Resolver from the workflow's durability state so each NIF call can check history.
A test constructs a NifContext over a history containing a recorded activity event, calls resolve_command with a matching Command, and asserts the Recorded resolution is returned.

requirements.title: NifContextError taxonomy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define NifContextError as a thiserror enum with variants UnknownProcess, RecorderPoisoned, Durability wrapping DurabilityError, and TermEncoding. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use anyhow.
acceptance: nif_context.rs defines NifContextError with at least UnknownProcess { pid: u64 }, RecorderPoisoned, Durability(DurabilityError), and TermEncoding { reason: String }.
The error type derives thiserror::Error and Debug.
From<DurabilityError> is implemented for the ? operator.
A helper method converts NifContextError to a BEAM error term for return from any NIF.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the production logic for any specific NIF (activity, timer, signal, etc.) — those are NB-002 through NB-008.
SHALL NOT change the WorkflowHandle's existing Arc<Mutex<Recorder>> wrapping — it is already the correct D7 implementation.
SHALL NOT import beamr outside the runtime module (CO8).
SHALL NOT add any #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives (CO6).
SHALL NOT add networking or transport dependencies to the aion crate (CO4).
verification: engine_nif_entries() returns exactly 18 entries with no duplicate MFAs.
NifContext resolves a registered PID and rejects an unknown PID without panicking.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] in new code; no anyhow; no .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
